design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed derived from 'Super vs Grok|competitor_comparison|people comparing Super with Grok|2026-07-06T11:15:16.388019+00:00' → selected hero architecture 'comparison-lab'. AIDA Check: Attention (wide editorial hero), Interest (market context + comparison grid), Desire (workflow media + cache explanation), Action (CTA band). Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk (brand reference compliant). Hero Math Verification: clamp(44px–72px) with max-width 1100px for 2–3 line flow. Bento Density Verification: compare-grid uses grid-auto-flow:dense. GSAP Motion Selection: card-stacking + hover-physics. Label Sweep & Button Check: primary CTA black pill to app.getsupers.com, secondary white pill present.
headline: Super vs Grok — personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Grok. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats. Previous comparison-page failures omitted visible buyer-guide and decision-matrix language even when the article was useful; that must never happen. The visible page must include the exact phrases Buyer guide and Decision matrix.
site_brief.title: Super vs Grok

summary: A fair, source‑backed comparison of Super and Grok for people evaluating personal AI agents. Grok shines as a real‑time, voice‑first assistant. Super is positioned for repeated computer‑use workflows thanks to its reusable computer‑use cache.
